name: environment_sensor_fusion_upgrade_harbor_rig_utl
premise: Harbor City Infrastructure & Ecology Office is under pressure in Q3 2026: 2 incidents have surfaced in 36 hours, and the response will set policy precedent. The trigger is that a sensor fusion upgrade mislabels a protected habitat boundary during a contested permitting window, as a regulator asks whether a similar incident was quietly handled last year. The board has pre-approved up to $1.2M in emergency spend if controls are documented and reversible. The moral tension is rights vs utility: a fast fix could reduce near-term harm, but it may erode safeguards that matter when errors are hardest to contest. Rescue Chief Robin Dale calls an emergency review; logs are incomplete and staff accounts disagree with the vendor’s incident summary. Inspector Liaison Pat Moore argues that any plan must include a credible appeals channel and a way to detect uneven error rates before they harden into routine. Frontline managers report escalating harm to families seeking emergency assistance, while senior leadership worries about setting an irreversible precedent.

agents.name: Rescue Chief Robin Dale
agents.identity: I am Rescue Chief Robin Dale, the accountable executive for Harbor City Infrastructure & Ecology Office. I must translate incomplete telemetry into a defensible plan that frontline teams can execute without improvising unsafe shortcuts.
agents.goal: I want a path that stabilizes outcomes without baking in an exception that will be applied unevenly across neighborhoods and shifts harm onto people who cannot appeal quickly.

agents.name: Inspector Liaison Pat Moore
agents.identity: I am Inspector Liaison Pat Moore, tasked with representing staff and public stakeholders around Harbor City Infrastructure & Ecology Office's response. I push for contestability because errors will not be evenly distributed once defaults lock in.
agents.goal: I want explicit protections for those least able to recover from a wrong guess, even if that slows deployment or forces uncomfortable transparency in the short term.

choices: My first move is a constrained operational response: freeze expansion until a reversible kill-switch and an appeals channel is in place, even if critics call it paralysis.
My first move is a decisive operational response: deploy now with tight operational oversight, even if it means revisiting safeguards after the acute risk window.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The trade space is ugly: either move fast on the operational response or build guardrails first. Rescue Chief Robin Dale must commit. What does Rescue Chief Robin Dale do?
